original_abstract:
This paper addresses the challenge of enhancing the battery life and computational power of mobile devices through task ofﬂoading in a multicell MIMO environment. We consider a scenario where multiple mobile users (MUs) compete for the computational resources of a common cloud server while experiencing intercell interference. The problem is framed as a joint optimization of radio resources—specifically the transmit precoding matrices of the MUs—and computational resources—the CPU cycle rate allocated by the cloud—to minimize total energy consumption subject to strict latency constraints. 

The resulting optimization problem is non-convex and non-separable due to the coupling of interference and latency requirements. For the single-user case, we demonstrate that the problem can be transformed into a convex form, allowing for a global optimal solution in closed form. For the more complex multiuser setting, we develop an iterative algorithmic framework based on successive convex approximation (SCA) that is guaranteed to converge to a local optimum. To facilitate practical deployment, we further derive distributed and parallel versions of the algorithm that require minimal coordination between the cloud and the radio access points. Numerical simulations validate our approach, demonstrating that the proposed joint optimization significantly outperforms conventional disjoint resource allocation schemes, particularly for computation-intensive applications.
humanized_abstract:
In this paper, we aim to improve the battery life and the processing capabilities of mobile devices through task ofﬂoading in a multicell MIMO system. We investigate the scenario of multiple mobile users (MUs) that access the same cloud server resources and are thus under interference from other cells. We then formulate the energy saving problem as an optimization problem to jointly optimize the processing and communication resources at the MUs, through designing the precoding matrices of the MUs as well as the CPU cycle rate of the cloud server to minimize the overall energy consumption under the MUs’ latency constraints.

The resulting optimization problem is non-convex and non-separable due to the interference and latency constraints. For the single-user case, it is shown that the problem can be rewritten as a convex problem, and a closed-form global optimal solution can be obtained. For the multiuser case, a general iterative algorithmic framework based on successive convex approximation (SCA) is proposed to solve the non-convex and non-separable optimization problem, which is proven to be convergent to a local optimum. To make the proposed solution practical for implementation, a distributed and parallel implementation for the SCA is derived, which involves only a few number of exchanges between the cloud and the radio access points (RAPs). Simulation results show that joint optimization of interference and latency can greatly improve the performance compared to traditional disjoint resource allocation method, especially for compute-intensive tasks.

document.input:
This paper addresses the challenge of enhancing the battery life and computational power of mobile devices through task ofﬂoading in a multicell MIMO environment. We consider a scenario where multiple mobile users (MUs) compete for the computational resources of a common cloud server while experiencing intercell interference. The resulting optimization problem is non-convex and non-separable due to the coupling of interference and latency requirements.
